title: Asperula apuana
intro: Asperula apuana, also known as the Apuanan wood sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate gastrointestinal issues and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Asperula apuana include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Asperula apuana is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to support digestive health and immune function.

Asperula apuana is native to the Mediterranean region and is typically found growing in dry, rocky areas.
